mnemonics: Pearson Correlates Pairs Perfectly (PCPP) - A mnemonic to remember that Pearson's correlation coefficient measures the strength and direction of a linear relationship between two variables (pairs).
keyPoints: Pearson's correlation coefficient (r) measures the linear correlation between two variables, resulting in a value between -1 and +1.
It is calculated as the covariance of the two variables divided by the product of their standard deviations.
A value of +1 indicates a perfect positive linear relationship, -1 a perfect negative linear relationship, and 0 indicates no linear correlation.
commonMistakes: Confusing correlation with causation; a high Pearson coefficient does not mean one variable causes the other.
Assuming a linear relationship is appropriate without first checking a scatterplot for the data's pattern.
